name: León City Tour – circular by the Carril del Torío and the historic center
name_original: León City Tour – circular por el Carril del Torío y el casco histórico
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around León traverse a diverse landscape, ranging from the high peaks of the Cantabrian Mountains to serene southern plains and rolling hills. The region features significant natural formations such as the Picos de Europa and the Babia Biosphere Reserve, offering varied terrain for cyclists. River valleys, including the Ribera del Órbigo, provide smoother paths, while areas like the Vegacervera Gorges present dramatic mountain rides with narrow roads and vertical walls. This varied topography supports a wide array of cycling experiences. Best touring cycling routes around León The most popular touring cycling route is León City Tour – circular by the Carril del Torío and the historic center, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the city's historic core and follows the Carril del Torío. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plaza Mayor – Late Roman Wall of León loop from Asunción-Universidad, an easy 17.4 miles (28.0 km) path. This route guides cyclists past historic landmarks, including the ancient Roman Wall of León. Local touring cyclists also love the La Candamia Park – View of León from Las Lomas loop from León, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail leading through La Candamia Park and offering views from Las Lomas, often completed in about 3 hours 2 minutes. Touring cycling around León is defined by mountain passes, river valleys, and historic cityscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging ascents. The routes in León are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.97 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore León's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around León?
A: León offers incredibly diverse terrain for touring cyclists. You can find routes traversing the high peaks of the Cantabrian Mountains, serene southern plains, and rolling hills. Notable natural features include the majestic Picos de Europa and the Babia Biosphere Reserve. For dramatic landscapes, the Vegacervera Gorges feature narrow roads and vertical walls, while the Ribera del Órbigo provides smoother, more accessible paths along the river.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in León?
A: Yes, León has options for all skill levels, including easy routes perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032857544/leon-city-tour-circular-by-the-carril-del-torio-and-the-historic-center" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León City Tour – circular by the Carril del Torío and the historic center&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 16.2-mile (26.0 km) route exploring the city's historic core. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31829038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor – Late Roman Wall of León loop from Asunción-Universidad&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 17.4-mile (28.0 km) path guiding cyclists past historic landmarks.

Q: What are some challenging touring cycling routes in the León region?
A: For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, León's mountainous regions offer demanding ascents. The Cantabrian Mountains feature passes like Pajares, Ventana, and Vegarada. The Babia Valley Loop in the Babia Biosphere Reserve is known for its difficult terrain. Additionally, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33901898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Candamia Park – View of León from Las Lomas loop from León&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33894645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum – La Candamia Park loop from León-Padre Isla&lt;/a&gt; are rated as difficult, offering significant elevation gain.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around León?
A: There are over 110 touring cycling routes available around León on komoot, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include 15 easy routes, 62 moderate routes, and 34 difficult routes, ensuring a suitable option for every cyclist.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: León is rich in history and culture. Many routes pass by significant landmarks. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5658871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman City Walls of León&lt;/a&gt;, visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;, or see Antoni Gaudí's &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4590991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also a crucial stop on the historic Camino de Santiago, offering routes through ancient towns like Astorga and Ponferrada.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in León?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in León are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032857544/leon-city-tour-circular-by-the-carril-del-torio-and-the-historic-center" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León City Tour – circular by the Carril del Torío and the historic center&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31829038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor – Late Roman Wall of León loop from Asunción-Universidad&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33901898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Candamia Park – View of León from Las Lomas loop from León&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in León?
A: While winters can be cold with snow, the relatively cool temperatures in summer make León an appealing destination for cycling compared to many other inland Spanish cities. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant weather conditions, making them ideal for touring cycling.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in León?
A: The touring cycling routes in León are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.97 stars from over 80 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain passes to serene river valleys, and the opportunity to explore historic cityscapes and cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The varied topography of León provides numerous opportunities for stunning views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33901898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Candamia Park – View of León from Las Lomas loop from León&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas of the city. The Picos de Europa and the Cantabrian Mountain passes also provide breathtaking scenery and elevated viewpoints.

Q: Can I find routes that incorporate the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, León is a significant stop on the Camino de Santiago (French Way). Cycling sections of this iconic pilgrimage route from León offers a unique experience, combining historic towns, charming villages, and scenic landscapes. The Camino features a mix of flat paths, rolling hills, and some steep climbs, particularly as you head towards O Cebreiro.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the ancient Roman gold mines of Las Médulas?
A: While not directly listed in the provided routes, the region of León is home to Las Médulas, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. This ancient Roman gold mining complex offers spectacular panoramic views from the Mirador de Orellán. Cyclists in the wider León region can plan routes to visit this unique historical and natural attraction.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on touring cycling routes in León?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route's difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032857544/leon-city-tour-circular-by-the-carril-del-torio-and-the-historic-center" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León City Tour&lt;/a&gt; have around 138 meters of elevation gain. Moderate routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38171199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of León Cathedral – Roman bridge loop from Puente Castro&lt;/a&gt;, feature about 191 meters. Difficult routes, like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33894645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Botines Gaudí Museum – La Candamia Park loop from León-Padre Isla&lt;/a&gt;, can have over 490 meters of ascent, reflecting the region's diverse topography.
tips:
The Plaza Mayor de León is the ideal place to finish a stage of the Camino de Santiago.
Sitting down to eat or have a coffee under the arcades of the square is the perfect way to end the efforts of the day.
From here, you can visit the Barrio Húmedo or Casco Antiguo and the Cathedral of León on foot.
On Wednesdays and Saturdays, the market is held in this square until 3:00 p.m.

Without a doubt, the Cathedral is the emblem of the city and its history begins in the 10th century. On top of the old baths and public buildings that the Legio VII had built in the middle of the 2nd century, stood the royal palace of Ordoño II. In 917, after defeating the Arabs in the battle of San Esteban de Gormaz, the king gave up his residence "gratefully to God" so that the first cathedral temple was erected there.

Above the entrance door there is a sculpture of Saint George killing the dragon. The building was designed for the weaving workshop on the ground floor and semi-basement, while the remaining four floors were used for rental apartments. Designed by the architect with a cubic shape, the four corners are highlighted with pointed elements.

waypoints.name: Access to Plaza Mayor de León
